parts.text: 4 (g) + H 2 O(g) CO(g) + 3H 2 (g) The reaction is carried out using a nickel catalyst at temperatures between 700 °C and 1100 °C and using a pressure of one atmosphere. The forward reaction is endothermic. (a) What is meant by the term catalyst? ........................................................................................................................................
parts.answer: M1 chlorine : treating (drinking) water / treating water in swimming pools / kill bacteria in water / chlorination of water / (manufacture of) paper products / plastics / PVC / dyes / textiles / medicines / antiseptics / insecticides / herbicides / fungicides / solvents / paints / disinfectant / bleach / hydrochloric acid; M2 sodium hydroxide : drain cleaner / oven cleaner / extraction of aluminium / purification of bauxite / (manufacture of) biodiesel /paper / soap / detergents / washing powder / textiles / dyes; M3 hydrogen : fuel / rocket fuel / fuel cells / in welding / (manufacture of) ammonia / NH / margarine / methanol / hydrochloric acid / refrigerants; 1 1 1 Page 7 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0620 42 OR (some) oxygen remaining; cm; 2 : 15 : 10 : 10; C H; 1 1 H; / triple bond / multiple bond; OR not all bonds are single bonds; test: aqueous bromine / bromine (water) / Br; result: (orange / yellow / brown) to colourless / decolourised / colour disappears; 1 1 propene: CH; polypropene: CH; 1 1 Page 8 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0620 42 / heat and in air / oxygen; → 2ZnO + 2SO; SO on right of equation; all formulae and balancing correct; M1 heat produced by carbon / coke (burning in) oxygen / air; OR C + O → CO produces heat / exothermic; OR 2C + O → 2CO produces heat / exothermic (scores M1 and M2 ); M2 C + CO → 2CO; OR 2C + O → 2CO; M3 ZnO + CO → Zn + CO; OR ZnO + C → Zn + CO; OR 2ZnO + C → 2Zn + CO; 1 1 1 / temperature (inside the furnace) is above the boiling point (of zinc) / °C is above the boiling point (of zinc); / condensing / condense;
